segments.text: Over the years, attitudes towards workers have changed considerably.
segments.translation: 近年来，关于工人的态度发生了巨大的转变。

segments.text: After all, there was a time when workers had no rights at all, and laboured in appalling conditions.
segments.translation: 毕竟，曾经有段时间工人没有任何权利。在恶劣的条件下劳动。

segments.text: Conditions have improved a lot, but conflict in the workplace is still common.
segments.translation: 虽然现在情况提升很多，但是工作中的冲突依然很常见。

segments.text: And human resources managers nowadays need to be able to deal with it when necessary.
segments.translation: 如今，人力资源经理需要在必要时有能力解决冲突。

segments.text: What is conflict in the workplace?
segments.translation: 什么是工作冲突？

segments.text: Definitions vary, but I'm taking it to refer to a whole range of behaviours that the victim finds unacceptable, from minor, harmless arguments to - at the opposite extreme - physical violence.
segments.translation: 各种定义不尽相同。但是我想用它来指代一种广泛的行为，受害者觉得不能接受的行为，从微小的无害的争论——到另一个极端——身体暴力。

segments.text: Much of this is covered by the term bullying, by which I mean one or more people behaving abusively or aggressively against another who is in a weaker position.
segments.translation: 大多数情况下可以用欺辱来形容。我的意思是一人或多人对抗居于弱势地位者，表现出辱骂行为或进攻行为。

segments.text: Although all behaviour like this is a form of conflict, not all conflict can be described in these terms.
segments.translation: 尽管像这样所有的行为都是一种冲突，但不是所有的冲突都可以用这些词来描述。

segments.text: As with all human behaviour, there are numerous reasons for it.
segments.translation: 正如所有的人类行为，都有很多原因解释。

segments.text: But often it's caused by someone who feels the need to show their superiority over someone else, in order to feel that they aren't at the lowest level in a hierarchy or a group of people.
segments.translation: 但是这种行为通常是由于某人想要展示高于别人的优越性而导致的，目的是感觉到他们在等级制度中，或一群人中不是位居最低的。

segments.text: In some cases one person simply dislikes the other, on the basis that the personality of one is in some way incompatible with that of the other person.
segments.translation: 在一些情况下，一个人不喜欢另一个人，是基于他的性格在某些方面与另一个人不相容。

segments.text: A general habit of optimism in one person could make them intolerant of a colleague who's constantly pessimistic - not that that justifies treating them badly, of course.
segments.translation: 一个乐观主义的人是不能容忍一个同事总是很悲观的——当然不是说对他不好。

segments.text: Some conflicts arise when people are more interested in promoting themselves and their team than in the company as a whole.
segments.translation: 有时一些冲突会产生，当人们更有兴趣提升自身或自己团队的时候，而不是公司整体。

segments.text: These conflicts are called 'structural', and could come about, for example, when a sales team believe they are the only people in the business who do any useful work, and look down on behind-the-scenes administrators.
segments.translation: 这些冲突叫做“结构性冲突”。例如当一个销售团队相信唯有他们是公司中在做有用的工作的人，并且轻视幕后管理者，这种结构性冲突就会发生。

segments.text: Conflict obviously affects the individuals concerned - the situation is likely to be very stressful for victims, resulting in their absence from work, possibly for months.
segments.translation: 冲突往往影响所涉及到的个人——比如说使受害者感到有压力，导致工作缺勤，甚至缺勤数月。

segments.text: For the company, if no effort is made to deal with conflict, it can spiral out of control, and even lead to the breakdown of the business.
segments.translation: 对于公司来说，如果不努力去处理冲突，公司会迅速失控，甚至倒闭。

segments.text: Some interesting work with chief executives - CEOs - has uncovered some of the reasons why they may treat colleagues badly.
segments.translation: 和首席执行官——CEO——共事的过程可以揭露他们为什么对待下属不好的原因。

segments.text: Many CEOs combine two opposing characteristics: confidence - that is, the belief that they're capable of great achievements - with a high level of anxiety, a fear of missing targets, whether set by themselves or by the directors of the company.
segments.translation: 很多CEO都对自身矛盾的性格。自信——一方面他们相信自己能获得巨大成就——伴随着高度焦虑。；而另一方面，又害怕错失目标，无论是自己设定的目标还是公司领导设定的。

segments.text: This combination can make them respond badly to anyone who questions their decisions.
segments.translation: 这种矛盾结合体使他们对任何质疑他们决定的人回应恶劣。

segments.text: In a high pressure work environment, such characteristics become problematic.
segments.translation: 在高压的工作环境中，这种性格会产生问题。

segments.text: And it's particularly difficult to tackle the situation where colleagues, managers and board members are all trying to achieve their own visions.
segments.translation: 尤其是当同事，经理，董事会成员都想实现自身利益的时候，这种情况非常难处理。

segments.text: When they can't agree on strategic issues and on where they see the business going, there are real problems.
segments.translation: 当对于公司的战略性问题和发展方向不能达成共识的时候，真的会有很大问题。

segments.text: For managers at lower levels within the organisation, it might seem that an autocratic form of management - where the chief executive gives orders and everyone else has to obey - would see more conflict than others.
segments.translation: 对于低级别的经理来说，公司更像是专制形式的管理——对于首席执行官给到的命令，其他人必须服从——所以这样的经理更容易看到冲突。

segments.text: Interestingly, though, a company with a more democratic business model can suffer more, when uncertainty about who to report to leads to conflicting demands.
segments.translation: 有趣的是，即便是一个更加民主的公司模式也可能会遭受更多冲突，比如说对于应该向谁汇报工作充满不确定性，从而导致相互冲突的要求。

segments.text: Now I'll say a little about dealing with the type of conflict that has harmful effects.
segments.translation: 现在我要谈一下如何解决冲突带来的负面影响。

segments.text: Of course the ideal is to prevent it arising in the first place.
segments.translation: 当然，首先，理想情况是在它发生之前就阻止掉。

segments.text: A good manager, at any level, will make efforts to earn the respect of the people they work with, particularly those who report to them.
segments.translation: 各级经理应该努力赢得同事的尊重，尤其是那些向他们做汇报的下级同事。

segments.text: That will involve politeness in all communications, and treating them as equals who happen to have a different role within the organisation.
segments.translation: 包括礼貌沟通，平等对待那些站在公司对立面的人。

segments.text: Sometimes, of course, conflict does occur, and can get out of hand.
segments.translation: 有时，当然，冲突一定会发生，甚至无法控制。

segments.text: In such cases the human resources department often gets involved.
segments.translation: 这种情况下，经常会涉及到人力资源部门。

segments.text: However, if one of the parties in a conflict sees human resources as simply a mouthpiece for the chief executive, then an external mediator might be able to help.
segments.translation: 然而，冲突一方把人力资源部看成是首席执行官的喉舌，那么就需要外部的调解者来协助。

segments.text: By talking to both sides, and trying to find the truth of what's been happening, they can build a clear picture of the situation, and give feedback that both sides will accept, precisely because they're independent.
segments.translation: 通过与双方沟通，试图发现事情真相，他们才能清楚的了解状况，并且给到双方都能接受的反馈，因为他们是互相独立的双方。
